analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (suggested direction.txt): counterparty-conditioned forward mids on Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4 trades days 1–3 joined to same-timestamp price rows. Horizons K∈{5,20,100} map to +K*100 timestamp units. Aggression: trade price vs concurrent L1 bid/ask. Spread regime: per-product tertiles on pooled tape. Script: r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended below (round4_phase1_complete). Phase 2 not started until sim/backtest validates top edges under worse fills.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Participant tables now loop **every distinct buyer/seller string** on the tape (R4 has only Mark IDs — no non-Mark counterparties). Strongest cell unchanged: Mark 67 aggressive buys on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT, k=5 self/extract (n=164, mean≈1.96, t≈13, CI excludes 0). **Per-day** (`r4_p1_headline_cells_by_day.csv`): M67|extract|buy_agg k=5 positive mean all three days (n 58/61/45, t>5 each); k=100 turns **negative on day 3** (mean≈−1.08, t≈−0.76) — long-hold extrapolation from k=5 is not day-homogeneous. **Cross-asset** at trade time (`r4_p1_participant_cross_forward_stats.csv`, n≥30 cells): forward **extract** and **hydro** mids from each (participant, side, trade_symbol, spread_bin) row; hydro is mostly weak |t| vs self-mid. Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300: negative k=5 self-mid (n=160). Mark 55 sell_agg on extract: horizon split as before. **Burst vs isolated** / **session**: same as prior iteration.

phase1_bullets.bullet: 2) Deviation from bot baseline (pair cell means + residuals)
phase1_bullets.conclusion: **Cell-mean baseline:** for each aggressive print, `cell_mean_dm_self_k20` = mean k=20 self-mid in its `(pair, symbol, spread_bin)` cell; per-print `residual_cell_dm_self_k20` is in `r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv`. **Dispersion ranking** (`r4_p1_top_cell_residual_dispersion_k20.csv` = same rows as legacy `r4_p1_top_residual_pairs_k20.csv`): hydro duopoly cells Mark14|Mark38 and Mark38|Mark14 on HYDROGEL_PACK|tight have highest within-cell residual std (~8.7 on ~500 prints) — large second-order variation inside the dominant pair×product regime. **Flow balance** (`r4_p1_participant_flow_balance.csv`): Mark01 net +4678 lots bought vs sold, Mark22 −5477, Mark67 +1510 (buy-only in tape), matches `r4_initial_analysis_summary.txt` roles.

Directed edge table matches repo sketch (Mark 01→Mark 22 top count). **Hub degree** (`r4_p2_graph_hubs_degree.csv`): Mark14 highest total edge-print mass (~2172), then Mark01/Mark22/Mark38 — consistent with hydro duopoly + basket pipe. **Reciprocity** (`r4_p2_graph_top_pairs_reciprocity.csv`): Mark14↔Mark38 ~0.98 min/max count ratio; Mark01→Mark22 has **zero** reverse prints (reciprocity 0) — pure one-way basket edge. Two-hop chains within 5k ts: Mark 38→14→38 n=885 mildly negative extract k=20. Mark 55→01→22 n=535: pooled dm_ex k5/k20/k100 means ≈ -0.21/-0.64/-1.39 (t≈-2.39/-3.44/-3.44) but day-stability is uneven — day1 k20 mean ≈ -0.10 (t≈-0.28, n=155); day2 k5 positive (+0.34, t≈3.0); day3 k20 ≈ -1.19 (t≈-4.0). Stateful sim trader_v8 (Sonic gate + arm on M55 buy extract from M01, short on next M01→M22 print in window) loses -11396 worse/all with 19 fills / 355 qty — execution timing differs from offline t2 anchor.

387 burst timestamps (~4.2 prints each). Dominant (buyer,seller) mode at each instant: Mark 01|Mark 22 on 318 bursts — mean extract dm_ex k20 ≈ +0.29 (vs pooled burst +0.33). Sonic tight covers ~86% of burst ts; tight bursts mean k20 ≈ +0.22 vs loose ≈ +1.0 (n=332 vs 55). Dominant M01|M22 bursts are not sign-stable by day: day1 mean k20 ≈ -0.14 (n=98), day2 ≈ +0.38 (n=96), day3 ≈ +0.57 (n=124) — orchestrator label refines basket bursts but does not yield a uniform short-horizon sign. **Matched-time control** (`r4_p1_burst_matched_control_ex_k20.json`): for each burst (day,timestamp), nearest same-day **isolated** price timestamp’s extract k=20; n=387 pairs, mean burst − control ≈ +0.13 extract units, **t≈0.79**, bootstrap 95% CI on the paired mean difference **crosses zero** (−0.18 to +0.44) — burst level extract uplift vs neighbor isolated times is not statistically distinguishable from noise in this crude match.

r4_p1_mark22_seller_markout_by_buyer_k20.csv aggregates Mark 22 aggressive sells across products — the Mark 49 tail (n=12, mean k=20 ≈ -2.71) is entirely VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (no Mark49 buyer on Mark22 voucher sells in this tape). sell_agg-only extract rows (n=11): pooled mean dm_self k5/k20/k100 ≈ -2.36 / -3.09 / -4.35 (t ≈ -2.66 / -1.93 / -3.14); per-day k=20 means are mixed (day2 small n positive). Sim trader_v6 (Sonic gate + short extract on M22→M49 aggressive sell) is negative under worse (-156) with only 3 fills / 10 qty — tail too sparse to scale. **Passive microprice:** `microprice_minus_mid` (L1 book imbalance proxy) is only populated for `agg==passive_mid` (n=3 on R4 days 1–3) — tape is almost entirely aggressive at touch, so per-Mark microprice conditioning is not empirically supported here.

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Follow Mark 67 aggressive buy prints on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT with short-horizon long bias (or avoid fading) at k=5
top_5_tradeable_edges.effect: mean Δmid ≈ +1.96 price units at k=5 on same symbol
top_5_tradeable_edges.evidence: r4_phase1_machine_summary.json; r4_p1_participant_forward_stats.csv; per-day k=5 in r4_p1_headline_cells_by_day.csv (days 1–3 all positive mean, n 58/61/45)

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Treat Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 as short-term bearish for 5300 mid (k=5)
top_5_tradeable_edges.evidence: r4_phase1_machine_summary.json; r4_p1_participant_forward_stats.csv; r4_p1_headline_cells_by_day.csv (k=5 self-mid negative days 1–3)

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Use same-timestamp multi-symbol bursts as extract risk-on / drift indicator (forward extract k=20)
top_5_tradeable_edges.evidence: r4_p1_burst_extract_fwd_k20.csv (caveat: r4_p1_burst_matched_control_ex_k20.json — burst vs nearest isolated timestamp paired difference not significant)

top_5_tradeable_edges.edge: Mark 55 on extract: separate buy vs sell horizons — aggressive buys mildly positive k=5/20; aggressive sells strongly negative k=100
Two-hop motif Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22 predicts negative extract forward (k=20) in bulk (n=535)

no_edge_or_negative_results: Mark 01 aggressive buy specifically on VEV_5300 at k=20: zero qualifying rows in tape slice (Mark 01→Mark 22 basket hits other lines); r4_p1_mark01_buy_vev5300_by_day_k20.csv empty — cannot claim participant alpha on that exact cell.
High-|t| two-hop tails (e.g. Mark 14→Mark 38→Mark 22) have n<20 — not stable as standalone rules.
Phase 1 is mid-only markouts; tradable PnL under worse fills not established (STRATEGY caveat applies).

Mark 22→Mark 49 extract adverse-selection tail: only 12 tape rows (11 sell_agg); per-day k=20 unstable (day 2 mean +2.3, n=3). trader_v6 gated short sim is negative under worse (-156) with 3 fills — not a scalable edge.

Mark 55 aggressive sells on extract: pooled k=100 mean ≈ -0.92 (t≈-2.5) but k=5/k=20 ≈ 0 / -0.11 (not short-horizon). trader_v7 (Sonic gate + short on each M55 agg sell tape print) loses -13671 worse with 600 contracts — high-frequency fade contradicts long-horizon mid evidence and accumulates adverse path PnL.

Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22 two-hop: offline extract drift negative pooled but day-1 k20 flat; trader_v8 stateful short under Sonic gate still -11396 (19 fills) — not a tradable translation of the graph motif.

Burst orchestrator (dominant buyer|seller per multi-print ts): Mark 01|Mark 22 dominates but extract k=20 forward means flip positive days 2–3 after slightly negative day 1 — not a monotone fade target without further conditioning.

Hydro duopoly (Mark38 agg buy HYDROGEL_PACK from Mark14): Sonic-tight rows show **positive** mean extract k=20 (+0.53, n=129) vs loose negative (-0.46, n=378) — contradicts using joint voucher gate to justify shorting extract on that flow; trader_v9 sim -14281.

Reverse hydro leg (M38 sell to M14 at bid): tight subset strongly positive extract k20/k100 offline but day3 mean k20 negative; trader_v10 long sim +11633 total yet still << 200k/day bar and day3 PnL disagrees with naive extrapolation from tight-only means.

Mark 22 VEV sell-pressure EMA + extract inventory trim (trader_v11 on top of v3) lowers worse/all totals vs v3 (+4764 / +5058 vs +5304 / +10953) with fewer taker fills — this MM-sketch is not a PnL upgrade at tested thresholds.

8-hour / full-day ‘session’ splits on Phase-1 participant tables are degenerate: `hour_cs` in r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv only 0,1,2 (example: Mark 67|extract|k=5 populates a single r4_p1_participant_forward_by_session.csv row) — not a disproof of time-of-day effects, a **data-coverage** limit for this run.

Burst vs nearest isolated-timestamp control on extract k=20 (387 pairs): mean paired difference burst−control ≈ +0.13, t≈0.79, bootstrap 95% CI crosses zero — naive ‘bursts predict extract drift vs matched quiet times’ is **not** significant at burst-clock resolution in this construction.

Round 4 trade CSVs only list **Mark** buyer/seller strings (7 buyers / 6 sellers in tape) — ‘each distinct name U’ is fully enumerated by the participant tables; there is no separate non-Mark counterparty universe in this dataset.

After subtracting the **cell-mean** baseline (pair×symbol×spread_bin), the naive summary ‘mean residual aggregated by pair only’ is ~0 by construction — second-order structure lives in **within-cell dispersion** (`r4_p1_top_cell_residual_dispersion_k20.csv`) and per-print residuals in `r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv`, not in pair-only means.

Joint max(VEV_5200, VEV_5300) spread vs |extract mid change| over 5 price rows: correlation ≈0.009 pooled and ≈0.004 when Sonic tight (`r4_p2_joint_spread_vs_extract_vol.csv`) — **no** microstructure edge for ‘spread compression predicts extract short-horizon vol’ on this grid.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2: orthogonal conditioning (burst ±500 ts around any Mark01→Mark22 print), Sonic joint gate on prices merged to enriched trades, lead–lag of Mark67 signed extract flow vs extract mid on full price grid, microprice/touch proxy when gate on. Script r4_phase2_analysis.py. Sim probe trader_v1 (Sonic gate + Mark67 aggressive buy on extract from market_trades) backtested worse+all.
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate object below. Phase 1 Mark67 extract edge survives per-day (k=5) but Sonic gate does not amplify it in the Mark67 subset (n small); burst neighborhood around Mark01→Mark22 does not change Mark67 mean. Lead–lag shows modest positive corr at 1–2 price rows. Post-fix (spread==1 execution + SUBMISSION-only fills summary): trader_v1 worse +2572 / all +3951 over days 1–3 — still far below 200k/day; prior +98 figure was wrong (v1 never traded on 1-tick extract).

phase1_interaction: Phase-1 headline (Mark67 aggressive buy extract → positive short-horizon mids) is confirmed per-day on k=5 (r4_p2_mark67_buy_extract_by_day_k5.csv). Conditioning on Sonic tight gate does not increase mean k=5 on that subset vs loose (r4_p2_sonic_gate_mark67_extract_k5.csv; n=27 tight vs 137 loose — refines Phase-1 by showing gate is not the dominant amplifier for Mark67 in this slice). Mark01→Mark22 burst neighborhood does not shift Mark67 k=20 extract forward mean (r4_p2_burst_pair_mark67_extract_k20.csv).

phase2_bullets.conclusion: Mark67|extract k=20 means identical with/without ±500ts neighborhood of Mark01→Mark22 prints; burst slice n=16 too thin to claim synergy. **Pair × burst stratum** (`r4_p1_pair_forward_by_burst.csv`, n≥30): e.g. Mark01|Mark22 on VEV_5300 in **multi_print_burst** shows negative k=5 self-mid (mean≈−0.15, t≈−2.3, n=132). **Per-day** (`r4_p2_pair_burst_forward_by_day.csv`): same cell is **not** sign-stable — day1 k=5 self_mid mean≈−0.28 (t≈−2.0, n=32), day2 k=5 mean≈−0.05 (|t|<0.5), day3 k=5 mean≈−0.13 (t≈−1.45) — pooled burst stat overstates out-of-sample day homogeneity; mean-revert vs trend split depends on horizon/day (e.g. day3 k=100 extract positive with large t but day2 k=100 extract ~0).

When joint gate is on, VEV_5200 rows are already at spread≤2 by construction; file records share_le2≈1.0 — inner-quote opportunity still limited to tape discretization. **Joint voucher spread vs extract short-horizon vol** (`r4_p2_joint_spread_vs_extract_vol.csv`): corr(max(5200,5300) spread, |Δextract mid| over k=5 rows) is **~0.009** pooled and **~0.004** in sonic_tight_only (n≈30k / 7.7k) — no evidence that wider joint surface predicts larger 5-row extract moves in this discrete panel (not a compression→vol edge).

phase2_bullets.bullet: 3) Cross-instrument lead–lag (Mark67 signed flow vs extract)
phase2_bullets.conclusion: Mark67 grid: lags 1–2 price rows (~100–200 ts) corr ~0.12 / ~0.095 vs signed M67 extract flow (decays at longer lag). **Multi-mark** signed extract notional vs next-row extract Δmid (r4_p2_leadlag_signed_flow_by_mark.csv): M67 lags 1–5 still strongest (corr ~0.06–0.12); M22 lags 1–5 ~0.02–0.04; M55/M49 ~0.003–0.013; M01/M14/M38 have **zero pooled flow variance** on extract (no aggressive prints on that symbol in R4) so corr is undefined — lead–lag is only meaningful for names with extract tape participation. Hydro duopoly (Mark38 agg buy hydro from Mark14, n=507): pooled extract k20 ≈ -0.21 (weak |t|); **tight** subset mean k20 **+0.53** vs **loose -0.46** — inverts a naive short under gate (trader_v9 -14281). Reverse leg (Mark14 buyer, Mark38 seller — all sell_agg, M38 hitting bid, n=496): pooled k20 ≈ -0.12 (n.s.); **tight** mean k20 **+1.08** (t≈2.62, n=126), k100 **+3.19** (t≈4.32) vs **loose** k20 **-0.53** (t≈-2.33). trader_v10 (gate + long extract on that hydro print) +11633 worse/all (36 fills, 600 qty) — profitable vs v9 but day3 sim negative (-3044) while offline day3 k20 for this slice is ≈ -0.80 (t≈-2.41); path PnL ≠ mid-only table.

Tight gate subset mean k=5 slightly lower than loose for Mark67 buys (2.0 vs 1.78 n.s. given n) — falsifies 'gate always cleans Mark67 signal' for this cell; Phase 3 still warranted for fuller interaction.

VEV_5000 has only 3 trade rows in Phase-1 enriched tape — cannot build seller×IV grid. Instead: at each of 317 unique (day,timestamp) with Mark22 seller on any VEV, fit quadratic IV in log-moneyness (10 strikes, BS bisection, T=(5-day)/365). Steepness q25/q50/q75 ≈ 0.070/0.078/0.089; 99.65% of Mark22 voucher sells coincide with sonic_tight. Top vs bottom steepness quartile: mean dm_self_k20 differs trivially on VEV_5300 (-0.50 vs -0.33, n 37/48) and ~0 on deep wings (6000/6500). Sim trader_v5 = v3 + require steepness≥q75 cal offline → identical PnL/fills to v3 (filter never binds when M67 fires).
After fixing join-bid on 1-tick books (buy at ask when bid+1>=ask), gated Mark67-follow extract is +2572 worse / +3951 all over days 1–3 (11 SUBMISSION fills worse); still no 200k/day scale.

Implemented a minimal sketch on top of v3: EMA(6) of per-timestep count of Mark 22 aggressive VEV sells (price<=bid) under Sonic tight; if pos_extract>0 and EMA>0.5 and pos>80, skip new Mark67 lift buys and (cooldown) limit-sell 18 at bid to trim long into that flow. Sim trader_v11: +4764 worse / +5058 all over days 1–3 vs v3 +5304/+10953 — the overlay cuts worse/all PnL and trade count (27 buys +4 sells worse, 320 extract qty) without reaching scale.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Lag-1–2 price rows: Mark67 signed extract flow positively correlates with subsequent extract mid drift
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Supports Phase-1 directionality but at different aggregation (flow grid vs print events).

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Mark67 aggressive buy extract — per-day stability of positive k=5 forward extract mid
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: mean k=5 extract forward ~1.75–2.2 ticks per day
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Confirms Phase-1 top cell is not a single-day artifact.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Sim: gated long extract after Mark67 lift (executable prices) is modestly profitable in aggregate
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Mid-only t-stats overstate sim PnL; sign still positive on this slice.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Sonic gate × Mark67 (negative interaction in mean k=5) — candidate for Phase-3 three-way tests
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: tight mean < loose mean on small n
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Refines / partially falsifies naive stacking of Sonic gate on Mark67 prints.

top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.edge: Burst neighborhood around Mark01→Mark22 does not improve Mark67 extract forward — avoid over-conditioning
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.effect: near-burst vs not identical mean k=20
top_5_tradeable_edges_phase2.relation_to_phase1: Orthogonal negative on basket-burst narrative for Mark67 overlay.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 3 (suggested direction.txt): re-run STRATEGY.txt Sonic joint gate on R4 aligned panel; spread–spread correlation; gate-conditioned extract forwards; merge gate onto enriched trades; three-way (gate × pair × symbol) k=20; Mark22 VEV_5300 sells by gate; burst×gate; Mark67 extract buys by gate; overlap counts. Script r4_phase3_sonic_gate_analysis.py. Sims: trader_v2 (wings), trader_v3 (Mark67+gate lift ask), r4_summarize_submission_fills.py for auditable own-fill counts.
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate object below. Sonic tight regime reproduces positive extract forward k=20 vs loose on R4 panel; three-way CSV guides sim v2 (negative PnL under worse). v3 (Mark67+gate, lift ask, larger clip) is best sim so far (+5304 worse) but still orders of magnitude below 200k/day.

phase3_bullets.bullet: STRATEGY.txt Sonic gate on R4 (aligned panel)
phase3_bullets.conclusion: Joint tight gate: positive extract Δmid k=20 mean vs loose (tape-level replication of R3 community claim on R4 days 1–3).
Enables three-way conditioning without re-parsing raw CSV in downstream scripts.
Motivated trader_v2 (Mark01←Mark22 on VEV_6000/6500 under gate); sim shows -900 worse/all (consistent with wing flow not translating to long-hold edge).
Most Mark22 5300 prints occur under tight gate in sample; markout split by gate is tabulated for future fades.
Structured burst risk under tight vs loose recorded for sizing overlays.
Subset sizes small; used with Phase-2 note that k=5 gate interaction on Mark67 is not a clean amplifier — v3 sim tests executable lift instead.

trader_v3 (Sonic gate + Mark67 agg buy + lift ask, clip 24): +5304 worse, +10953 all over days 1–3; trader_v5 (add IV smile steepness≥q75): same totals/fills as v3; trader_v2 wings: -900; trader_v4 (Mark22 5300 fade): -3054 worse. Fills summaries count SUBMISSION as buyer or seller.

phase3_sim_headline.best_trader: trader_v3.py (trader_v5 ties: same worse/all PnL and fills)
phase3_sim_headline.gap_to_target: 200k/day × 3 days would be 600k total; best observed ~0.9% of that under worse fills.
phase3_sim_headline.cross_phase_note: trader_v10 (Phase2 hydro leg + same Sonic gate) +11633 worse over days 1–3 — higher PnL than v3 but driven by hydro counterparty, not Mark67 voucher flow. trader_v11 (Phase2 bullet-7 M22-sell pressure + inventory trim on v3) +4764 worse / +5058 all — underperforms v3; keep v3 as Mark67+gate reference unless MM skew is re-tuned.

analysis_description: Post–Phase-3 evidence pass: day-stability table for Mark 22 aggressive sells on VEV_5300 (agg=sell_agg) from r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv — horizons k∈{5,20,100} by day and pooled. trader_v4 sim (Sonic gate + fade at bid on that tape signal). Extended r4_summarize_submission_fills.py to count seller==SUBMISSION for short fills.
used_to_decide: k=5 negative mean on Mark22|5300|sell_agg is stable days 1–3 (n 38/44/78; t≈-2.1,-2.3,-1.4). k=20 turns weakly positive by day 3 (mean +0.013, n=78) so a static short-hold fade is horizon-mismatched. trader_v4 (path-dependent inventory) loses -3054 worse / -2063 all with 54 sell fills and 900 contracts traded — mid-only edge does not survive this execution overlay.

analysis_description: Phase 2 follow-up (deferred bullet 5): IV smile proxy × Mark 22 voucher flow. Script r4_phase2b_iv_smile_mark22.py builds r4_p2b_smile_at_m22_sell_ts.csv, r4_p2b_m22_sell_join_smile.csv, r4_p2b_steep_vs_fwd_k20_by_symbol.csv, r4_p2b_machine_summary.json. trader_v5.py stacks offline q75 steepness threshold on trader_v3; backtests worse+all.
used_to_decide: Smile steepness at Mark22 voucher sells is high and concentrated (mean ~0.079); quartile spread in forward k=20 markouts on VEV_5300 is small vs noise. IV×counterparty filter does not down-select Mark67+gate extract entries in sim (v5 ≡ v3 PnL).

analysis_description: Phase 1 adverse-selection disambiguation: Mark 22 → Mark 49 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT only (12 rows in r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv). Script r4_mark22_mark49_extract_evidence.py emits r4_p1_m22_m49_extract_markout_by_day.csv and r4_p1_m22_m49_extract_markout_summary.json (horizons k∈{5,20,100}, sell_agg vs all, by day). trader_v6.py: Sonic gate + market_trades M22→M49 aggressive sell on extract → limit sell at bid; backtests worse+all.
used_to_decide: Offline sell_agg slice supports short-horizon down-mid on extract after M22→M49 prints, but day-2 k=20 subset is positive (n=3) and n is tiny. Gated sim still loses under worse (-156, 3 submission sells) vs -438 all — execution and rarity dominate any fade.

analysis_description: Phase 1 Mark 55 on VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT: pooled and per-day forward mids for buy_agg (buyer==M55) vs sell_agg (seller==M55) at k∈{5,20,100}. Script r4_mark55_extract_evidence.py → r4_p1_mark55_extract_by_day.csv, r4_p1_mark55_extract_summary.json. trader_v7.py: Sonic gate + short extract (limit sell at bid) on each tape print where Mark 55 aggressively sells extract; backtests worse+all.
used_to_decide: Buy-side M55 is mildly positive at k=5/20 (t≈2.2/2.5). Sell-side k=100 negative (t≈-2.5) with per-day k=100 means -0.48/-1.40/-0.88 (day 2 strongest). Naive high-frequency short fade on every sell print under gate is strongly negative in sim (-13671 worse, 29 fills, 600 qty) — horizon mismatch vs Phase-1 headline.

analysis_description: Phase 1 two-hop deep-dive: Mark 55→Mark 01→Mark 22 extract forwards at horizons k∈{5,20,100} with per-day t-stats; event-level CSV. Script r4_twohop_m55_m01_m22_detail.py. trader_v8.py: stateful Sonic-gated sim (arm on M55 aggressive buy extract from M01; fire short extract at bid on first subsequent M01→M22 print within 5000 ts); backtests worse+all.
used_to_decide: Pooled two-hop extract drift is negative at k=5/20/100 (n=535), but day 1 k=20 is not significant and day 2 shows positive k=5. Sim shorts on first basket print after arm lose heavily (-11396, 19 submission sells, 355 qty) — motif is not robust as a simple trigger under worse fills.

analysis_description: Phase 1 burst orchestrator attribution: at each multi-print (day,timestamp), count buyer/seller names across all symbols; dominant pair = argmax counts (ties broken lexicographically). Join extract dm_ex_k20; merge Sonic gate from r4_p3_trade_enriched_with_gate.csv. Script r4_burst_orchestrator_analysis.py. results.json iteration 9 records analysis-only (null backtests); no new trader_vN.
used_to_decide: Dominant Mark01|Mark22 bursts (n=318) have mild positive mean extract k=20 (+0.29); day-split shows sign flip (day1 slightly negative, days 2–3 positive). Sonic-tight burst subset remains positive on average (+0.22). Suggests basket bursts are not a clean short-extract trigger without additional state; complements Phase 3 burst×gate extract table.

analysis_description: Phase 2 cross-instrument: HYDROGEL_PACK Mark38|Mark14 aggressive-buy prints — extract forward mids by day and by Sonic gate (r4_hydro_duopoly_extract.py). trader_v9.py: Sonic gate + market_trades M38 agg buy hydro from M14 → short extract at bid; backtests worse+all (identical totals this run).
used_to_decide: Pooled hydro→extract k=20 is only mildly negative; gate-conditioned means flip positive in the tight subset (n=129). Sim shorting extract on each gated hydro signal is strongly negative (-14281) — do not stack Sonic gate as endorsement for this cross-product fade.

analysis_description: Phase 2 hydro reverse leg: Mark14 buyer / Mark38 seller on HYDROGEL_PACK (Phase1 agg all sell_agg — M38 aggressive sell at bid). r4_hydro_reverse_pair_extract.py -> r4_p2c_hydro_m14_m38_summary.json, r4_p2c_hydro_m14_m38_by_gate.csv. trader_v10.py: Sonic gate + that tape print -> long extract at ask (clip 18); backtests worse+all.
used_to_decide: Symmetric duopoly direction matters: M38→M14 bid hits associate with strong positive extract drift in tight gate (k20 +1.08, k100 +3.19, n=126) vs negative loose. Sim long +11633 (36 fills) beats v9 but day3 loses (-3044) while offline day3 k20 for this slice is negative — mid-only gate slice overstates tradable edge magnitude and sign stability intraday.

analysis_description: Phase 2 bullet 7 (inventory MM sketch): r4_phase3_incline_spread_panels.py fix — drop diagonal spread–delta correlations (1.0 artifacts) and pair each product spread with its own 1-tick mid change only; re-ran to refresh CSV/JSON. trader_v11.py: v3 + EMA(6) Mark 22 aggressive VEV sell intensity under gate; when long extract, high pressure, pos>80 — skip new lifts and (cooldown) sell 18 at bid; backtests worse+all; fills summary.
used_to_decide: Incline spread–price block no longer has spurious 1.0 self-corrs. Inventory-aware M22 overlay on Mark67+gate taker is worse than v3 in total PnL and fill volume — 'skew away from new risk when a repeated seller is active' is testable but this parameterization is not a near-term PnL upgrade vs baseline v3.

analysis_description: Plan-spec gap-fill (Phase 1 + Phase 2 cross-instrument, no new sim). Extended r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py: `r4_p1_participant_forward_by_session.csv` (Mark × side × symbol × spread_bin × session_bin H00_07/H08_15/H16_23, horizons k∈5,20,100). Re-ran Phase 1. New r4_phase2_leadlag_multi_mark_flow.py → r4_p2_leadlag_signed_flow_by_mark.csv (signed extract notional per Mark vs next extract mid, lags 1–20 price rows; documents zero-variance marks). Updated phase bullet conclusions for session degeneracy and multi-mark lead-lag evidence.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 “session stratify” is now explicit evidence: 8h buckets do not sub-split the dominant M67|extract|k5 cell in this R4 cut (only 3 `hour_cs` values exist). Lead–lag table shows M67 remains the dominant extract flow signal on grid; M01/M14/M38 have no extract aggressor variance so **corr undefined** in this construction — not ‘no lead-lag in general’ but no extract-local signed flow to correlate for those names.

analysis_description: Phase 1 plan gap-fill (no Phase 2/3 re-gates): r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py now adds median, bootstrap 95% CI on mean (400 resamples, seed 42) to summarize(); refreshes r4_p1_participant_forward_stats.csv and burst pooled CSV. New r4_p1_participant_forward_by_burst.csv (Mark×side×symbol×spread×burst_stratum×k). New r4_p1_burst_matched_control_ex_k20.json: paired extract dm_ex_k20 at each multi-print (day,ts) vs nearest same-day isolated price timestamp. Re-ran Phase 1 script.
used_to_decide: Participant-level reporting now matches plan wording (median + CI + burst stratum). Matched-control burst test weakens treating raw burst timestamps as a clean extract alpha source vs isolated tape times; headline M67/M22 participant cells unchanged in sign but CIs are auditable in CSV.

analysis_description: Phase 1 exhaustive counterparty enumeration + cross-asset + day stability: participant loops use every distinct buyer/seller string on tape (R4 = Marks only). New r4_p1_participant_cross_forward_stats.csv (forward extract and hydro mids from each trade row, n≥30 per cell). New r4_p1_headline_cells_by_day.csv (Mark67 extract buy_agg, Mark22 VEV_5300 sell_agg, Mark55 extract sell_agg — per day self/extract/hydro k∈5,20,100). Updated top_5_tradeable_edges evidence paths and burst caveat. Re-ran r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py.
used_to_decide: Plan text ‘each U’ is satisfied by construction (only Mark names exist on tape). Cross-forward table shows hydro/extract co-moves are mostly secondary vs self-mid for voucher-focused signals. M67 k=100 on day 3 flips negative — refines rank-1 edge as **short-horizon** not unconditional long-hold.

analysis_description: Phase 1 bullet 2 + Phase 2 pair×burst: r4_phase1_counterparty_analysis.py adds cell-mean baseline for k=20 self-mid (pair×symbol×spread_bin), per-print residual_cell_dm_self_k20 in r4_p1_trade_enriched.csv, r4_p1_participant_flow_balance.csv (qty net per name), r4_p1_top_cell_residual_dispersion_k20.csv (top within-cell residual std). r4_p1_pair_forward_by_burst.csv: (pair,symbol,spread_bin)×burst_stratum×horizon for self_mid and extract (n≥30). microprice_minus_mid for passive_mid prints (n=3). Updated phase1 bullet 2/5 and phase2 bullet 1 conclusions.
used_to_decide: Cell-mean baseline makes naive pair-only residual means ~0; dispersion table surfaces hydro duopoly and extract pipe cells as high within-regime variance. Pair×burst file gives Phase-2-style conditioning without new script entrypoint.

analysis_description: Phase 2 extension in r4_phase2_analysis.py: r4_p2_graph_hubs_degree.csv (buy+sell edge mass per participant), r4_p2_graph_top_pairs_reciprocity.csv (count_ba + min/max reciprocity for top pairs), r4_p2_pair_burst_forward_by_day.csv (pair×symbol×spread×burst×day×horizon mean/t/n for self_mid and extract), r4_p2_joint_spread_vs_extract_vol.csv (corr max/min joint voucher spread vs |extract Δmid| over k=5 rows, pooled/tight/loose/by day). Updated analysis.json Phase1 bullet 3 and Phase2 bullets 1–2; added no_edge line on spread-vol.
used_to_decide: Graph reciprocity quantifies Mark14↔Mark38 two-way hydro vs Mark01→Mark22 one-way basket. Day-split burst basket stats falsify a simple ‘always fade 5300 on burst’ rule. Joint spread–extract vol correlation ~0 kills a naive Phase-2 microstructure compression hypothesis without sim.
